composition: installed exact local package tarballs rendered through native framework component APIs

unknowns: portal.container: Autocomplete.Content uses Base UI Portal without exposing a container prop; exact portal target under provider/shadow DOM was not asserted.
ssr.popup: Closed action-free SSR is supported; open portal markup and generated ids depend on Base UI and were not frozen as exact strings.
defaultFiltering: Canonical composition with explicit items does not filter automatically in this fixture; ArrowDown selects Apple after typing Ban.
tooltipTimingAndIconIdentity: Tooltip timing and arbitrary icon component rendering are delegated to Tooltip and React and are not guessed by this contract.
inheritedBaseUIEventDetails: Callback detail object and browser/platform edge behavior are inherited from Base UI and are intentionally not guessed.

inheritedVendorBehavior: Base UI event details, autofill/password managers, IME, RTL, selection ranges, clipboard rejection, and platform accessibility-tree differences remain inherited/vendor unknowns.
generatedIds: Base UI generates input/listbox ids; relationships are required but literal ids are intentionally not frozen.
multipleChipDom: Exact nested chip/input DOM depends on renderItem and controlled value; behavior is specified without inventing a universal fixture shape.
closedRootPlaceholder: A controlled closed portal may yield no rendered element; shared runner needs a mount-host convention before root expectations can distinguish host from component output.
selectionOnPlainEnter: Canonical wrapper documents selection but Item click ownership remains consumer-provided; exact event log is not asserted.
ssrOpenPortal: Action-free closed SSR is supported; exact open portal output and generated ids are Base UI implementation details.

localizedLabelsAndCalendarText: Calendar strings, week layout, and locale-specific formatting are owned by react-day-picker and Intl/browser locale; this contract intentionally does not invent locale behavior.

calendarMonthAndLocalizedDayLabels: The component initializes from the current local month and generates labels with browser locale APIs; exact dates and strings are deliberately not frozen or invented.
alertdialogEscape: Declarations guarantee outside-click resistance but do not state Escape policy; delegated browser evidence was not available for an exact claim.
generatedIds: Title/description association ids are generated by Base UI and literal values are not stable contract surface.
vectorRootPortalConvention: Open content is portaled and the shared runner's root selection convention may require adapting the alertdialog vector without changing canonical semantics.
vectors.pointer-item-selection: Required browser vector is authored but pending the shared browser adapter; event order and close-on-select are not claimed as passing.
vectors.keyboard-typeahead-submenu-dismissal: Required keyboard/typeahead/submenu vector is pending shared browser execution.

dismissalAndEventOrdering: Exact outside-event phase, cancellation, link activation, close-on-select defaults, callback order, and focus restoration are delegated to Base UI and not directly observed.
positioningPortalHydration: Collision fallback, RTL, nested-layer arbitration, custom container behavior, portal relocation, deterministic IDs, and hydration node preservation need browser evidence.
typeahead: Matching normalization and timeout are Base UI behavior and were not directly observed.

inheritedVendorBehavior: Base UI event details, autofill/password managers, IME, RTL, selection ranges, clipboard rejection, and platform accessibility-tree differences remain inherited/vendor unknowns.

inheritedVendorBehavior: Base UI event details, autofill/password managers, IME, RTL, selection ranges, clipboard rejection, and platform accessibility-tree differences remain inherited/vendor unknowns.

inheritedVendorBehavior: Base UI event details, autofill/password managers, IME, RTL, selection ranges, clipboard rejection, and platform accessibility-tree differences remain inherited/vendor unknowns.

inheritedVendorBehavior: Base UI event details, autofill/password managers, IME, RTL, selection ranges, clipboard rejection, and platform accessibility-tree differences remain inherited/vendor unknowns.
semantics.rootWhenRenderProvided: The caller-provided React element controls the polymorphic root.
semantics.rootWhenRenderProvided: The render prop or LinkProvider controls the framework-specific root.
vectors.buttons-active-index: Options require ReactNode icons and function callbacks not representable by the serializable fixture DSL; shared browser fixture execution is required.
vectors.active-by-id: ID matching and arrow wrap vector is authored as a requirement but pending shared browser execution.
tooltip: Tooltip portal DOM, accessible description relationship, delays, hover/focus dismissal, and hydration are outside the MenuBar chunk and unobserved.
focusLifecycle: Listener behavior during focus movement, dynamic option changes, disabled descendants, and focus-in/out ordering require browser execution.
responsivePositioningTypeahead: MenuBar has no responsive, portal, positioning, selection typeahead, or dismissal API of its own; only nested Tooltip may portal/dismiss.

selectPopupPlatformPresentation: Dropdown popup geometry, native accessibility projection, and keyboard details are inherited from the Kumo Select/Base UI implementation and browser; they are not guessed here.
vectors.pointer-open-close-outside: Portal positioning, outside interaction, dismissal ordering, ARIA association, and focus restoration require the pending shared browser adapter.
vectors.controlled-open: Controlled open callback semantics are a required browser vector and are not fabricated from SSR.
positioning: Collision fallback order, arrow placement, available-size variables, RTL, zoom, viewport edges, virtual anchors, and fixed versus absolute coordinates need direct browser observation.
dismissalFocus: Exact outside pointer/focus phase, preventDefault behavior, nested layers, initial-focus policy, Tab behavior, and restoration ordering are delegated to Base UI.
portalHydration: Custom/provider/body container precedence is declared; SSR open markup, portal relocation, generated ID stability, warnings, and node preservation remain unexecuted.
collisionPlacement: Base UI may flip the requested side at viewport boundaries; this Chrome fixture observes bottom when top collides.
inheritedBaseUIRovingFocus: RTL wrapping, Home/End, and browser-specific roving-focus edge behavior inherited from Base UI are not declared by Kumo and are not guessed.
existingSelectPilot.react-vue: proof/select/receipts.json records React and Vue blocked before meaningful interaction because harness queries returned null; their interaction gates remain blocked.
existingSelectPilot.svelte: Existing receipt records failed pointer open/select, typeahead, controlled value/open, and callback ordering. These failures remain required parity gates.

existingSelectPilot.solid: Existing receipt records failed pointer open/select, typeahead, controlled state, callback ordering, hydration, and focus/scroll. These failures remain visible and unresolved.

portalPositioningHydration: Anchor-width minimum, available-height scrolling, collision behavior, provider/custom/body portal precedence, open SSR, portal relocation, generated IDs, warning-free hydration, and node preservation require direct evidence.

crossBrowserStylesAssets: Required Chromium, Firefox, WebKit, RTL, zoom, reduced motion, mobile/coarse pointer, stylesheet presence, icon loading, error/loading/Field variants, and animation reversal vectors have not run.
diacriticTypeahead: Locale and diacritic normalization are inherited from Base UI; the executable vector uses deterministic ASCII typeahead.

inheritedVendorBehavior: Base UI event details, autofill/password managers, IME, RTL, selection ranges, clipboard rejection, and platform accessibility-tree differences remain inherited/vendor unknowns.

ssrHydration.responsiveBranch: SSR always evaluates isMobile=false, while the first client render reads matchMedia synchronously. A narrow viewport can therefore choose desktop aside on the server and mobile portal/nav on hydration; no identical-hydration or browser-pass claim is made.

inert.ssrSerialization: Mobile, CollapsibleContent, and SlidingView apply/remove inert imperatively in ref callbacks. aria-hidden is SSR-observable, but inert presence before/through hydration must be browser-observed and is not asserted action-free.
mobile.portalAndFocus: Exact portal placement, focus restoration ordering, and focusout interactions require a browser; behavior is documented from canonical runtime but no browser pass is claimed.

CollapsibleTrigger.fixture: CollapsibleTrigger requires a render ReactElement. The generic JSON composition DSL cannot encode a ReactElement-valued prop, so trigger linkage is not fabricated in the action-free vector.

SlidingViews.direction: The public direction prop is declared and defaults left, but canonical runtime computes translation solely from active child index; no direction-dependent observable effect is invented.
semantics.rootWhenRenderProvided: The caller-provided React element controls the root.
inheritedBaseUIFormSemantics: Reset/autofill and platform activation details inherited from Base UI are outside the canonical Kumo declaration and are not guessed.

render.customElementSemantics: Item render clones a consumer element with anchor-like props; its resulting tag, router behavior, and focus semantics depend on that supplied element and are not invented.
consumer.activeStatePolicy: Canonical TableOfContents performs no observation or scroll tracking; which item is active and whether multiple active flags are supplied is wholly consumer-controlled.
overflow.pointerGeometry: Whether a given label set overflows and exact scrollLeft changes depend on measured browser layout, fonts, and viewport; no browser pass is claimed.
baseUi.generatedIds: Base UI may emit generated identifiers and additional state attributes; their exact values are implementation-managed and are intentionally not frozen.
panels: Canonical Tabs 2.5.2 has no panel API and renders no tabpanel; panel lifecycle, aria-controls linkage to consumer content, and content visibility must not be invented.

announcementTimingAndRemovalDelay: Assistive technology announcement timing and animation-completion removal timing are inherited from Base UI, CSS, and the user agent; exact wall-clock timing is not guessed.
